newsSinceJun6: No new company-specific news since June 6. Today's -5.4% move (to ~$277.75, ~14% below the May 11 all-time high of $321.94) tracks a sector-wide pullback in AI data-center electrical names (Vertiv -12% on the week, Eaton ~-4.8%, nVent ~-4.7%) and looks flow-driven, not fundamental. The known fundamentals are intact and strong: record $1.80B backlog, $490M Q2 orders, and the post-quarter >$400M first-phase greenfield data-center order (largest in company history) - all disclosed before June 6 and reflected in the June 8 verdicts.

sources.label: Simply Wall St - POWL record Q2 backlog and AI data center deal
TIKR - Vertiv fell 12% this week (AI power complex pullback)
MacroTrends - POWL stock price history (May 11 ATH $321.94)

verdicts.rankRationale: On this crowding-sensitive alpha list, a -5.4% flow-driven day with the thesis fully intact (record backlog, $400M+ data-center order) and ~14% drawdown from the May high marginally restores asymmetry that the +136% YTD rerating had destroyed; small nudge up, not a re-thesis.
verdicts.bull: Switchgear remains a genuine bottleneck in data-center power delivery; record $1.8B backlog and the largest order in company history give multi-quarter revenue visibility, and the pullback offers a slightly better entry.
verdicts.neutral: The story is now well known and the stock sits in the 'crowded/rerated benchmark' bucket; a one-day -5.4% only partially unwinds a +136% YTD run, so residual alpha stays modest.
verdicts.bear: If AI capex sentiment keeps rolling over, high-beta rerated power names fall hardest; multiple compression can continue even with backlog intact, and order lumpiness invites a guidance air pocket.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Follow-on phases of the >$400M greenfield data-center award and continued record order intake in Q3 FY2026.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued de-rating of the crowded AI-power complex independent of fundamentals.

verdicts.rankRationale: On the network-centrality lens nothing changed: Powell's bottleneck position in data-center electrical rooms is unaffected by a flow-driven -5.4% day, and the record backlog/$400M order supporting node centrality were already in yesterday's hold.
verdicts.bull: Electrical switchgear capacity is scarce and Powell's backlog plus the largest-ever order confirm it is a real, hardening node in the AI power-delivery chain.
verdicts.neutral: Centrality is real but no longer hidden; the market already recognizes the node, so conviction stays Medium with no new information since June 6.
verdicts.bear: Customer concentration in a few hyperscale projects means a paused or rescoped campus could dent the backlog narrative more than the node framing suggests.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Capacity expansion updates and additional data-center electrical-room awards converting backlog into accelerating revenue.
verdicts.primaryRisk: AI data-center buildout digestion slowing order intake from its record pace.
